title: LASER at SCNU
text:
Welcome to LASER
We are a research group in the Department of Chemical Engineering at Sunchon National University. LASER develops computational and experimental workflows that help complex engineered systems become more autonomous, reliable, and data-efficient.




Research Focus

Autonomous Process & Experiment Design: Value-of-information–driven sequential design and active learning for data-efficient engineering (DOE/iDoE)
Decision Engineering: Constraint- and uncertainty-aware optimization for design and operation, including sustainability assessment
Operation: Operational strategy and implementation for complex engineered systems (including control, monitoring, and scheduling)

Learn more →




Recent Publications

Taeseung Choi - Concentration-regulated operation of an electrified TSA-based direct air capture unit for greenhouse CO2 enrichment, Applied Chemistry for Engineering, 2026 [LINK]


Jiyun Kim - Direct Data-Driven Controller Design Using Fictitious Reference and Regression, Computers and Chemical Engineering, 2026 [LINK]

See all publications →




Quick Links

Publications

About & Contact

Contact: khryu@scnu.ac.kr










NoteWe’re Recruiting!



We welcome highly motivated graduate students interested in autonomous systems engineering, process modeling and optimization, and data-efficient learning for complex engineering systems.
Learn how to join →

title: About LASER
text:
LASER (Laboratory for Autonomous Systems Engineering Research) is a research group in the Department of Chemical Engineering at Sunchon National University, Republic of Korea. The lab is led by Prof. Kyung Hwan Ryu.
Designing and operating complex engineered systems often relies on sustained expert experience and know-how across experiment design, operation, and decision-making. LASER asks a single question: how much of that reliance can be reduced through principled, verifiable computation?
Our research connects experimentation, modeling, optimization, monitoring, and decision-making under physical constraints and uncertainty. We are particularly interested in data-scarce and high-consequence settings where physical consistency, uncertainty awareness, and reliable assessment matter. Our work spans chemical, electrochemical, and bioprocess systems.

title: About LASER
text:
Joining LASER
We welcome inquiries from students and researchers interested in autonomous engineering, process systems, and data-efficient scientific computation.

Researchers & Postdoctoral Fellows
We welcome applicants with backgrounds in process systems engineering, modeling and optimization, control, uncertainty-aware decision-making, or scientific machine learning. Applicants from related quantitative disciplines, including Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Applied Mathematics, Data Science, and Biology, are also encouraged to apply if they are interested in complex engineered systems.


Graduate Students (M.S. / Ph.D.)
No prior research experience is required. We primarily look for intellectual curiosity, a solid mathematical or engineering foundation, and a genuine interest in solving problems through computation. Research in the lab broadly includes autonomous experimentation, modeling and optimization under uncertainty, and monitoring and control of complex engineered systems.

title: About LASER
text:
How to Apply
Please send the following to khryu@scnu.ac.kr:

CV or resume
Transcript (for graduate applicants)
Brief statement of research interests (1 page or less)
Any relevant publications, reports, or research experience (if available)







NoteProspective Graduate Students



For formal graduate admissions information at Sunchon National University, please also visit the university website.

title: Research
text:
AUTONOMOUS SYSTEMS ENGINEERING

LASER develops computational workflows that help engineers determine what to investigate, which alternative to select, and how to operate complex systems under uncertainty and limited data.
Our research connects autonomous design, decision engineering, and operation into closed-loop engineering workflows grounded in physical knowledge, data, and verifiable computation.

Physical consistency Data efficiency Uncertainty awareness Verifiability

title: Research
text:
Integrated Research Framework




LASER studies closed-loop workflows in which experiments and models inform engineering decisions, decisions are implemented in real systems, and new observations improve subsequent design and operation. Open the full-size figure

title: Research
text:
Research Themes




01


Autonomous Design

What should be investigated, modeled, or designed next?

Autonomous design addresses how experiments, models, and design spaces can be progressively refined with limited data and computational resources. We develop methods that select informative next actions while respecting physical constraints and uncertainty.
Core methods

Sequential and Bayesian experiment design
Active learning and value of information
Surrogate and hybrid modeling
Design-space exploration


Representative workSequential experimental design · Piecewise response-surface modeling







02


Decision Engineering

Which design, technology, or operating alternative should be selected?

Decision engineering studies how alternatives should be evaluated when objectives conflict, predictions are uncertain, and models are incomplete. The goal is not only to identify an optimum, but also to make the basis and reliability of the decision transparent.
Core methods

Optimization under uncertainty
Multi-objective decision-making
Techno-economic assessment
Environmental and sustainability assessment


Representative workUncertainty-penalized optimization · Sustainable blue hydrogen







03


Operation, Monitoring & Control

How should the system be operated and adapted over time?

We develop implementable methods for systems affected by disturbances, model mismatch, changing operating conditions, and limited process knowledge. Our work connects control-oriented modeling, direct data-driven control, monitoring, and operational decision support.
Core methods

System identification and control-oriented modeling
Direct data-driven controller design
Monitoring and diagnosis
Scheduling and operational strategy


Representative workDirect data-driven controller design · Robust process identification

title: Research
text:
Application Domains
Our methods are developed and tested through engineering problems in which data are costly, physical constraints are important, and decisions have long-term technical or environmental consequences.



Carbon Capture, Separation & Utilization
Membrane gas separation, CO₂ capture and utilization, purity–recovery–energy trade-offs, and process performance and cost assessment.




Electrified & Low-Carbon Process Systems
Electrified reboilers and thermal systems, process-utility electrification, operational strategy, and decarbonization assessment.




Hydrogen & Energy Systems
Hydrogen storage and transport, power-generation process operation, renewable-integrated systems, dynamic optimization, and scheduling.




Bio-, Electrochemical & Emerging Systems
Bioprocess and electrochemical-system modeling, autonomous experimentation, technology evaluation, and sustainability assessment.

title: Research
text:
Current Research Directions




01


Closed-Loop Experiment–Model–Decision Workflows
Integrating experiment design, model updating, uncertainty assessment, and next-action selection into a unified iterative workflow.






02


Reliable Data-Driven Operation
Developing implementable control and monitoring methods that remain reliable under model mismatch, limited data, and changing operating conditions.






03


Autonomous Low-Carbon Process Systems
Connecting process design, sustainability assessment, and adaptive operation for membrane, electrified, hydrogen, and energy systems.






04


Model and Method Selection
Developing systematic frameworks for selecting models, experimental strategies, surrogates, and optimization methods according to available information and engineering objectives.

title: LASER Website Update Log
text:
Fix publication page: remove href=" data-original-href=" artifacts before [LINK]
Fix paper [27] (kim2025piecewise) - authors showing as “—.” instead of actual names
Members page: 1-column layout with photo (left) + info (right), load photos from ./Info/Images/picXXX
Members page: Load from student.csv, categorize by Status (Graduate->Alumni, MS/PhD->Grad Students, Post-Doc), hide empty sections
Home page: Auto-load 2 recent publications from journal.bib where first author is lab member
Add Board/News page tab
Create history folder for tracking updates







author-format.lua: Fixed author parsing regex. The old regex ([^a][^n][^d]-) was fundamentally broken for splitting author lists on ” and “. Replaced with proper find() + sub() loop that correctly handles all author names.
publications.qmd (JavaScript):

Fixed em-dash detection: Added \s* to handle leading whitespace in innerHTML (/^\s*---\./)
Added replacedEmDash flag to skip first-author format conversion on em-dash-replaced entries (prevents “Jiyun Kim” from being incorrectly rearranged)
Fixed DOI cleanup: Changed regex order to first remove the full https://doi.org/<a href="...">text</a> pattern before cleaning standalone URLs. This fixes the href=" data-original-href=" artifact caused by partial regex matching.






member-loader.lua: Lua filter reads student.csv and generates member sections:

Categorizes by Status: Graduate->Alumni, MS->M.S. Students, PhD->Ph.D. Students, Post-Doc->Post-Doctoral
Empty sections are automatically hidden (no Post-Doc or PhD sections shown if no members)
Student photos loaded by index from ./Info/Images/ with auto-extension detection
Generates proper Pandoc Header elements for TOC support

members.qmd: Redesigned. PI section stays hardcoded. Graduate students + alumni generated from CSV via member-loader.lua filter. 1-column layout with photo left, info right.
home-loader.lua: Lua filter for index page:

Reads student.csv to build lab member name list (PI + all students/alumni)
Reads journal.bib entries in order
Finds first 2 papers where first author matches a lab member (fuzzy 3-char given name matching)
Generates formatted output: Given Family - Title, Journal, Year [LINK]

index.qmd: Recent Publications section now auto-loads from journal.bib via home-loader.lua
board.qmd: New Board/News page using Quarto listing feature. Posts in board/ directory.
board/welcome.qmd: Sample welcome post.
**_quarto.yml**: Added Board tab to navigation navbar.
styles.css: Added member card styles (.member-card, .member-photo, .member-info).






Lua filters (run at build time during quarto render):

author-format.lua - Parses journal.bib, injects JSON data as <script> for publications page JS
project-loader.lua - Parses projects.bib, generates project tables
member-loader.lua - Parses student.csv, generates member cards and alumni table
home-loader.lua - Parses journal.bib + student.csv, generates recent publications

JavaScript (runs in browser on publications page):

Post-processes citeproc output: sorts by year, adds index numbers, fixes em-dash authors, converts first author format, bolds PI name with authorship markers, cleans DOI and adds [LINK]

CSS (styles.css): Custom styles for all page components
Data files:

Info/Bib/journal.bib - 32 journal publications with authorship/DOI fields
Info/Bib/projects.bib - 17 projects (ongoing/completed)
Info/student.csv - Student data (Index, Status, Name, Research, Email, Phone, Joined, Graduation, Thesis, Position)
Info/Images/ - Photos (Pic000.jpg for PI, pic002.jpg, pic003.jpg for students)






Home: Hero section + 3 cards (Research Focus, Recent Publications auto-loaded, Quick Links)
About: Lab intro, contact info (W3-305, 061-750-3588), Join section
Research: Research areas
Projects: Tables from projects.bib (5 ongoing, 12 completed)
Publications: Year-grouped, indexed, bold PI name with markers, DOI [LINK]
People: PI + Education/Experience, Graduate Students from CSV with photos, Alumni table from CSV
Board: Quarto listing page for news/updates









Publication page: Year-based grouping, index numbers, bold PI name, authorship markers
Member page: PI info, education, professional experience
Projects: Separate tab with bib-based data
Name changes: “LASER Lab” -> “LASER”, “Advanced” -> “Autonomous”
About: Office W3-305, Phone 061-750-3588
Various formatting and content updates





Created full site structure with Quarto
Set up publication page with JavaScript post-processing for citeproc output
Created author-format.lua for bib data injection
Created project-loader.lua for projects.bib parsing
Created projects.bib with 17 projects
Updated all pages with lab information

Prof. Kyung Hwan Ryu





Associate Professor Department of Chemical Engineering Sunchon National University
Email: khryu@scnu.ac.kr | Office: D3-305 | Phone: 061-750-3588
Research Interests: Process Systems Engineering, Carbon Capture & Utilization, Process Optimization & Control, Sustainable Energy Systems
